item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really dislike the excessive use of pseudoscience babble on this one, to the point of downvote. Scranton whatever all over the place, classification for reality benders, assumptions about what plot-powers cannot do, just to throw them up immediately for plot purposes.

This article is solid, with a good narrative, without those devices, they are just weighting things down.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad you enjoyed it! Fixed the typo also - completely forgot those were two different words :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this a lot. Definitely something I haven't seen before.

I feel like the Foundation mindset here is, "Well, shit… I guess we've still got to keep Procedure 44-Red-Tacoma on file, though, right?"

(Also, you've got a "seemly" in the logs which I assume was meant to be "seemingly")

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Then I succeed, as those were some of the objetives I had while drafting this up haha. Glad you liked it, I'll try to remain consistent in future articles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad you liked it! That's pretty much what I was going for - a skip that is actually about other non-mainlisters. I owe FFB for the Gamma\Delta idea, the original draft had blackboxes for both skips and was even more confusing :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You made me feel sympathy for SCP-GAMMA. Poor reality bender, never wanted to hurt anyone, certainly never wanted to be REDACTED by SCP-DELTA.

Very clearly described, which helped. It read like something that would happen in the Foundationverse, just another reason to be grateful for not living there

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Gamma and Delta designations confused me a tad, but ultimately I think it just means the specific identities of those objects is not necessary to know to understand this article. Quite a neat journal of its creation, and of course I really like articles where skips exhibit unexpected behavior and then shit goes pear-shaped. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a solid first entry with a good containment breach story attached. Well done.

+1

item.title: Credits
item.content:encoded:
Here we go. Big thanks to FortuneFavorsBold, Tuomey and InitHello for the very helpful feedback.

e: Props to Bentu for that typo!
